prompt_md: A high-volume background job classifies millions of records per month. Analysis shows about **85%** of records are easy and a small, cheap model labels them correctly with high confidence, while the rest are genuinely ambiguous and need the large model. The team currently sends **every** record to the large model and wants to cut cost **without losing quality on the hard cases**. Latency is not user-facing. **Pick the best design decision and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Send every record to the large model but cap the output to one token
Use a cascade: the small model labels first, escalating to the large model only when its confidence check fails
Send every record to the small model only and accept whatever it returns
Batch all records and send them all to the large model in one big request
reason_choices: Because most records are easy, so paying the large-model price only on the escalated minority lowers average cost while the hard cases still reach the large model
Because capping output to one token removes the need for a large model entirely
Because the small model is always as accurate as the large model, so quality cannot drop
Because batching changes the per-token price charged by the provider
